title: Correcting Incorrect CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Row Height Calculation and Variable Item Count

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes a CSS grid layout definition as a string and returns the corrected grid layout definition with consistent row height calculation and variable item count. The function should handle cases where the grid layout definition contains multiple rows with different heights and variable number of items. The corrected grid layout definition should be in the same format as the input string.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the CSS grid layout definition.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS grid layout definition as a string.
statement.notes: The grid layout definition should be in the format 'grid-template-columns: column1 column2 column3; grid-template-rows: row1 row2 row3;'. The corrected grid layout definition should have consistent row height calculation and variable item count.

constraints.description: The number of rows in the grid layout definition
The number of columns in the grid layout definition

examples.explanation: The input grid layout definition has consistent row height calculation and variable item count, so the output is the same as the input.
The input grid layout definition has inconsistent row height calculation, so the output is the corrected grid layout definition with consistent row height calculation and variable item count.

hints.text: First hint text: Check the grid layout definition for consistent row height calculation and variable item count.
